heading: National Law Enforcement and Corrections Technology Centers
label: 165 National Law Enforcement and Corrections Technology Centers

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Director of the Office shall operate and support National Law Enforcement and Corrections Technology Centers (hereinafter in this section referred to as “Centers”) and, to the extent necessary, establish new centers through a merit-based, competitive process.
support research and development of law enforcement technology;
support the transfer and implementation of technology;
assist in the development and dissemination of guidelines and technological standards; and
provide technology assistance, information, and support for law enforcement, corrections, and criminal justice purposes.

children.heading: Annual meeting
children.label: c Annual meeting
children.text: Each year, the Director shall convene a meeting of the Centers in order to foster collaboration and communication between Center participants.

children.heading: Report
children.label: d Report
children.text: Not later than 12 months after , the Director shall transmit to the Congress a report assessing the effectiveness of the existing system of Centers and identify the number of Centers necessary to meet the technology needs of Federal, State, and local law enforcement in the United States.November 25, 2002
